name: JonDash official addons
description: Official add-on modules and helpers for JonDash. In JonDash, add this repo as a module source (Admin -> Modules -> Manage sources) to browse and install. Modules update independently of the JonDash base app. Channels: the main branch = stable; this manifest (beta branch) = beta. See VERSIONING.md.

modules.name: Health monitoring
modules.description: Watches your services with HTTP, TCP, ping, DNS and certificate checks, records uptime and response times, and alerts by email or webhook when something goes down.
modules.notes: Monitoring now starts with the server instead of when someone first opens the dashboard, so a restart overnight no longer leaves your services unwatched until morning. The advanced "how often to look for due checks" setting has been removed - it never controlled how often anything was actually checked.

modules.name: Module template (for developers)
modules.description: FOR DEVELOPERS — a working example to copy when building your own module. Installs to modules/template; open MODULE.md in that folder for a full guide, and AI-PROMPT.md to have an AI build one for you. Safe to install, and safe to uninstall when you're done.
modules.notes: The template depends on nothing again: the scheduler helper and its background job have been removed, so copying it gives you a module that installs on its own. Helpers are still documented as an option. Works on far more versions of JonDash than before.

helpers.name: Scheduler
helpers.description: Runs modules' declared background work on time, starting when the server starts rather than when someone opens a page.
helpers.notes: Same runtime as 0.0.1-beta.1, with minAppVersion corrected to the 1.5.0 pre-release. Proven end to end: a declared job ran at server start with nobody signed in, and was skipped while its module was disabled.

helpers.name: Files and folders
helpers.description: Lets a module copy and archive folders to another location — a network share, an external drive — within the folders you allow.

provides.label: Look at files and folders in the locations you allow
Create and change files in the locations you allow
Delete files in the locations you allow

helpers.notes: First release. Copies and mirrors folders within locations an administrator approves, and nothing else — it exposes no way to read a file's contents. sync and snapshot modes only; deletion is not yet built. The first helper to name its own capabilities.
